nodes.title: Happy warmth
nodes.snippet: Gelid light and air were in the kitchen but out of doors gentle summer morning everywhere. Made him feel a bit peckish. / Cold oils slid along his veins, chilling his blood

nodes.title: Right. right.
nodes.snippet: three, four : right. She didn't like her plate full. Right.

nodes.title: Licking the saucer clean
nodes.snippet: dull and squat, its spout stuck out / licking the saucer clean / Thin bread and butter

nodes.title: They understand what we say
nodes.snippet: They call them stupid. They understand what we say better than we understand them.

nodes.title: Seem to like it
nodes.snippet: Seem to like it. / bending his senses and his will, his soft subject gaze at rest. The crooked skirt swinging, whack by whack by whack. / A soft qualm, regret, flowed down his backbone, increasing. Will happen, yes. Prevent. Useless: can't move.

nodes.title: Wonder is it true
nodes.snippet: Wonder is it true if you clip them they can't mouse after. / Why? They shine in the dark, perhaps, the tips. Or kind of feelers in the dark, perhaps. / Black conducts, reflects, (refracts is it?), the heat.

nodes.title: Short knock
nodes.snippet: Got a short knock. / make that corner in stamps / lost property office

nodes.title: Old Tweedy
nodes.snippet: old Tweedy. Yes, sir. At Plevna that was. I rose from the ranks, sir, and I'm proud of it. / old ranker too, old Tweedy's big moustaches

nodes.title: His hand
nodes.snippet: His hand took his hat from the peg / His hand accepted the moist tender gland and slid it into a sidepocket.

nodes.title: Card behind the headband
nodes.snippet: White slip of paper. Quite safe.

nodes.title: Seven Eccles street
nodes.snippet: On the doorstep / he turned into Eccles street, hurrying homeward

nodes.title: Footleaf
nodes.snippet: the footleaf dropped gently over the threshold, a limp lid

nodes.snippet: the steeple of George's church / The bells of George's church.

nodes.snippet: our daily / On earth as it is in heaven.

nodes.snippet: Night sky, moon, violet, colour of Molly's new garters.

nodes.snippet: Kind of stuff you read: in the track of the sun

nodes.snippet: the headpiece over the Freeman leader: a homerule sun rising up in the northwest from the laneway behind the bank of Ireland

nodes.snippet: a tramline along the North Circular from the cattlemarket to the quays / Those mornings in the cattlemarket

nodes.title: Simon Dedalus
nodes.snippet: Simon Dedalus takes him off to a tee with his eyes screwed up.

nodes.title: Russians
nodes.snippet: The Russians, they'd only be an eight o'clock breakfast for the Japanese.

nodes.snippet: redheaded curates from the county Leitrim

nodes.title: Passing a pub
nodes.snippet: Good puzzle would be cross Dublin without passing a pub

nodes.title: Joggerfry
nodes.snippet: Inishturk. Inishark. Inishboffin. At their joggerfry. Mine. Slieve Bloom.

nodes.title: Woods
nodes.snippet: Woods his name is. Wonder what he does. Wife is oldish. New blood. No followers allowed. / a constable off duty cuddled her in Eccles lane

nodes.title: Agendath Netaim
nodes.snippet: the model farm at Kinnereth on the lakeshore of Tiberias. Can become ideal winter sanatorium. Moses Montefiore. / Agendath Netaim: planters' company. To purchase waste sandy tracts from Turkish government and plant with eucalyptus trees.

nodes.title: Lost in the wood
nodes.snippet: O please, Mr Policeman, I'm lost in the wood.

nodes.title: Citrons
nodes.snippet: citrons / Must be without a flaw

nodes.title: Bleibtreustrasse
nodes.snippet: Bleibtreustrasse 34, Berlin, W. 15. / Nothing doing. Still an idea behind it.

nodes.title: Pleasant old times
nodes.snippet: Wonder is poor Citron still alive in Saint Kevin's parade. And Mastiansky with the old cither. Pleasant evenings we had then. Molly in Citron's basketchair. / Moisel told me. Arbutus place: Pleasants street: pleasant old times.

nodes.title: Cloud cover
nodes.snippet: A cloud began to cover the sun / Quick warm sunlight came running

nodes.title: Dead Sea
nodes.snippet: Brimstone they called it raining down: the cities of the plain: Sodom, Gomorrah, Edom.

nodes.title: Wandering Jew
nodes.snippet: Wandered far away over all the earth, captivity to captivity

nodes.title: Salt cloak
nodes.snippet: age crusting him with a salt cloak

nodes.title: Number eighty
nodes.snippet: Number eighty still unlet. Why is that? Valuation is only twenty-eight. Towers, Battersby, North, MacArthur: parlour windows plastered with bills.

nodes.title: Smoke of tea
nodes.snippet: To smell the gentle smoke of tea, fume of the pan, sizzling butter. Be near her ample bedwarmed flesh. / The warmth of her couched body rose on the air, mingling with the fragrance of the tea she poured.

nodes.title: Infinite of space
nodes.snippet: in slim sandals, along the brightening footpath / a girl with gold hair on the wind

nodes.title: Mullingar
nodes.snippet: Mullingar. / Might take a trip down there.

nodes.title: Tam
nodes.snippet: tam / Everyone says I'm quite the belle in my new tam.

nodes.title: You are my darling
nodes.snippet: O, Milly Bloom, you are my darling. / You are my lookingglass from night to morning. / I'd rather have you without a farthing

nodes.title: Old Professor Goodwin
nodes.snippet: Poor old professor Goodwin. Dreadful old case.

nodes.title: Breasts
nodes.snippet: her large soft / bubs, sloping within her nightdress like a shegoat's udder

nodes.snippet: Là ci darem / Voglio e non vorrei.

nodes.snippet: the orangekeyed chamberpot / the broken commode

nodes.title: Metempsychosis
nodes.snippet: Metempsychosis, he said, frowning. It's Greek: from the Greek. That means the transmigration of souls.

nodes.snippet: The first night after the charades. Dolphin's Barn.

nodes.snippet: Ruby: the Pride of the Ring.

nodes.title: Trapeze
nodes.snippet: Trapeze at Hengler's. / Had to look the other way. Mob gaping. Break your neck and we'll break our sides. Families of them. Bone them young so they metempsychosis.

nodes.title: Capel Street library
nodes.snippet: Must get that Capel street library book renewed or they'll / write to Kearney, my guarantor.

nodes.snippet: The Bath of the Nymph over the bed.

nodes.title: Slimmer
nodes.snippet: Not unlike her with her hair down: slimmer.

nodes.snippet: There is a young student comes here some evenings named Bannon his cousins or something are big swells

nodes.snippet: song about those seaside girls / Not a bit funky. Her pale blue scarf loose in the wind with her hair. / Pier with lamps, summer evening, band

nodes.snippet: He liked to read at stool. / piles

nodes.title: Titbits
nodes.snippet: an old number of Titbits / Our prize titbit

nodes.title: Sing a song of sixpence
nodes.snippet: The maid was in the garden. / The king was in his countinghouse.

nodes.title: Far east
nodes.snippet: Want to manure the whole place over, scabby soil. A coat of liver of sulphur. All soil like that / without dung. Household slops. Loam, what is this that is? The hens in the next garden: their droppings are very good top dressing. Best of / all though are the cattle, especially when they are fed on those oilcakes. Mulch of dung.

nodes.title: Bluebottle
nodes.snippet: That bee or bluebottle here Whitmonday.

nodes.snippet: bowing his head / under the low lintel

nodes.title: Philip Beaufoy
nodes.snippet: Mr Philip Beaufoy, Playgoers' Club, London

nodes.title: Cascara sagrada
nodes.snippet: Costive. One tabloid of cascara sagrada.

nodes.title: Matcham's Masterstroke
nodes.snippet: Neat certainly. Matcham often thinks of the masterstroke by which he won the laughing witch who now. Begins and ends morally. Hand in hand. Smart.

nodes.title: Dance of the hours
nodes.snippet: Ponchielli's dance of the hours. Explain that: morning hours, noon, then evening coming on, then night hours. / Evening hours, girls in grey gauze. Night hours then: black with daggers and eyemasks. Poetical idea: pink, then golden, then grey, then black. Still, true to life also. Day: then the night.

nodes.title: Quarter to
nodes.snippet: Heigho! Heigho! / Heigho! Heigho! / Heigho! Heigho!
